topic: Active learning and user adaptation in AI agents

sources.title: Toward Personalized LLM-Powered Agents: Foundations, Evaluation, and Future Directions
keyInsights: Personalization permeates the entire agent pipeline, not just output
Four capabilities: profile modeling, memory, planning, action execution
User signals must be propagated across the full agent decision pipeline

sources.title: Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents: Mechanisms, Evaluation, and Emerging Frontiers
keyInsights: Five memory families: context compression, retrieval-augmented, reflective, hierarchical, policy-learned
Memory is the differentiating factor in personalized agents
Memory operates via write-manage-read loop

sources.title: Do LLMs Recognize Your Latent Preferences? A Benchmark for Latent Information Discovery
keyInsights: Preference inference success varies from 32% to 98% depending on context
Tri-agent evaluation system (User, Assistant, Judge)
Effective preference inference remains an open frontier

sources.title: Towards Proactive Personalization through Profile Customization (PersonalAgent)
keyInsights: Lifelong personalization via sequential decision-making across turns
Unified user profile that evolves across sessions
Addresses cold-start and long-term personalization simultaneously

sources.title: Simulating Before Planning: Constructing Intrinsic User World Model
keyInsights: Diffusion model for dynamic user profile inference
Brownian Bridge-inspired anticipator predicts user reactions
Active learning prioritizes challenging user personas during training

sources.title: Enhancing Personalized Multi-Turn Dialogue with Curiosity Reward
keyInsights: Curiosity reward encourages active user characteristic inference
User model improvement as implicit feedback signal
Effective for new or context-limited users

sources.title: Principled Fine-tuning of LLMs from User-Edits
keyInsights: User edits combine preferences, supervised labels, and cost signals
Ensembling procedure for multiple feedback types outperforms single-type
Robustly adapts to different user-edit distributions at test time

sources.title: STEAM: Structured and Evolving Agent Memory
keyInsights: Atomic memory units per distinct interest dimension
Consolidation (refine) + Formation (new interests) mechanisms
Community-organized memories enable collaborative signal propagation

sources.title: Memoria: Scalable Conversational LLM Memory with Knowledge Graph
keyInsights: Hybrid: session-level summarization + weighted knowledge graph
Captures user traits, preferences, behavioral patterns as structured entities
Bridges stateless LLM interfaces and agentic memory systems

sources.title: PPP: Productivity, Proactivity, Personalization (Multi-objective RL)
keyInsights: +21.6 average improvement over GPT-4 on SW engineering and research tasks
Strategic questioning improves personalization outcomes
UserVille simulation environment for diverse preference testing

sources.title: PersonaAgent with GraphRAG
keyInsights: LLM-derived graph index with community detection for persona alignment
11.1% news categorization, 56.1% movie tagging improvements on LaMP
Combines individual behavior summaries with collective graph patterns

sources.title: MACF: Multi-Agent Collaborative Filtering for Recommendations
keyInsights: User and item agents collaborate dynamically via orchestrator
Dynamic recruitment: orchestrator selects similar user + relevant item agents
Differs from static preference aggregation in traditional CF

sources.title: SuperLocalMemory: Privacy-Preserving Multi-Agent Memory
keyInsights: 104% NDCG@5 improvement via adaptive learning-to-rank personalization
Bayesian trust defense: 72% trust degradation for delayed attacks
10.6ms median search latency, SQLite + FTS5 + Leiden clustering

sources.title: Illustrating Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)
keyInsights: Three stages: pretrain, reward model, PPO fine-tuning
KL divergence penalty prevents reward hacking
Explicit (ratings) vs implicit (engagement) feedback comparison
Open-source: TRL, TRLX, RL4LMs

sources.title: Memory for Agents (LangChain)
keyInsights: Three types: procedural (system prompt), semantic (facts), episodic (past actions)
Hot path vs background memory update tradeoffs
Memory is application-specific: coding agents vs research agents differ

sources.title: Mem0: The Memory Layer for AI Agents
keyInsights: +26% accuracy vs OpenAI Memory, 91% faster, 90% fewer tokens
Multi-level: user/session/agent memory tiers
LLM-based preference extraction with embedding-based retrieval
Supports 17+ integrations via MCP

sources.title: Zep: Temporal Knowledge Graph for Agent Memory
keyInsights: Temporal context graph with valid_at/invalid_at fact timestamps
Sub-200ms retrieval for production use
Tracks preference evolution over time, not just current state
Custom entity types per domain (sales leads, product interests)

sources.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
keyInsights: OS-inspired virtual context management: fast (in-context) + slow (disk) tiers
LLM controls memory paging via interrupts
Enables coherent multi-session interactions

sources.title: Do LLMs Recognize Your Preferences? PrefEval Benchmark
keyInsights: Preference following accuracy < 10% at 10 turns in zero-shot settings
Both explicit and implicit preference signals tested
Fine-tuning on PrefEval significantly improves preference following

sources.title: Language Model Personalization via Reward Factorization
keyInsights: User preferences lie in a low-dimensional space
~10 user responses sufficient for meaningful personalization
67% win rate over default GPT-4o responses in human eval

sources.title: One Adapts to Any: Meta Reward Modeling for Personalized LLM Alignment
keyInsights: MAML-style meta-learning: learns process of preference adaptation
Robust Personalization Objective emphasizes hard-to-learn users
Few-shot personalization for new users with minimal feedback

sources.title: PLUS: Learning to Summarize User Information for Personalized RLHF
keyInsights: Online co-adaptation: summarizer + reward model train simultaneously
11-77% improvement over standard approaches
25% better zero-shot on new users; interpretable user representations
72% win rate with GPT-4o

sources.title: Swap-guided Preference Learning for Personalized RLHF
keyInsights: Identifies posterior collapse problem in variational preference learning
Swap-annotation strategy preserves latent user preference representations
Submitted to ICLR 2026 - cutting-edge research

sources.title: MiCRo: Mixture Modeling and Context-aware Routing for Preference Learning
keyInsights: Single BT reward model has irreducible error from preference subgroups
Dynamic routing allocates users to appropriate reward models per context
Discovers preference clusters organically without manual annotations

sources.title: LoRe: Personalizing LLMs via Low-Rank Reward Modeling
keyInsights: Low-rank basis captures structure of diverse user preferences
Scalable few-shot adaptation to new users
Superior generalization to unseen users vs full reward models

sources.title: RLHF Fine-Tuning for Implicit User Feedback in Conversational Recommenders
keyInsights: Implicit signals used: dwell time, sentiment polarity, engagement patterns
PPO + weakly-labelled engagement info as reward model training data
Implicit signal alignment achieves scalable and user-adaptive design

sources.title: MTRec: Aligning Recommendations via Mental Reward Models
keyInsights: Clicks are noisy proxies - users click curiosity but experience discomfort
Distributional IRL models true user satisfaction vs observable signals
7% increase in average viewing time at production deployment

sources.title: Neural Contextual Bandits for Personalized Recommendation (Tutorial)
keyInsights: Matthew Effect: popular items get richer in training data
UCB bonuses force exploration of undersampled response strategies
Collaborative neural bandits balance user heterogeneity with correlations

sources.title: LLMs and Multi-Armed Bandits: A Component Survey
keyInsights: Bidirectional benefit: MABs help LLM personalization, LLMs improve MAB arm definition
MABs provide principled adaptive decision-making under uncertainty
First systematic survey of LLM-MAB interaction

sources.title: Hierarchical Contextual Uplift Bandits
keyInsights: Hierarchical: broad system insights → user-specific contexts
Contextual similarity enables cold-start policy transfer
Production: 0.4% A/B revenue improvement + 0.5% post-deployment gain

sources.title: From Guessing to Asking: Resolving Persona Knowledge Gaps in LLMs
keyInsights: +42% preference improvement in movie recommendations vs baselines
+27% in mental health support conversations
Most effective in extended dialogues (12+ turns)
CPER: Contextual Understanding + Dynamic Feedback + Response Generation

sources.title: Inside Out: Core Memory Trees for Long-Term Personalized Dialogue
keyInsights: PersonaTree: trunk (schema) + branches (domains) + leaves (preferences)
RL-trained MemListener generates {ADD, UPDATE, DELETE, NO_OP} operations
Comparable to DeepSeek-R1 on memory operation decisions with smaller model

sources.title: PersoDPO: Scalable Preference Optimization for Persona-Grounded Dialogue
keyInsights: Multi-LLM evaluation generates preference pairs without human annotation
DPO enables persona consistency without PPO infrastructure
Tested on FoCus dataset; consistent improvements over DPO variants

sources.title: HumAIne-Chatbot: Real-Time Personalized Conversational AI via RL
keyInsights: Dual signals: implicit (typing speed, sentiment, dwell time) + explicit (likes/dislikes)
Pre-trains on diverse virtual personas to establish broad prior
Adapts both content and style based on evolving user profile

sources.title: A Personalized Dialogue Generator with Implicit User Persona Detection
keyInsights: Infers user persona from dialogue history without explicit persona descriptions
Bidirectional: models user characteristics not just agent personality
Posterior-discriminated regularization for better user-awareness

sources.title: Less is More: Learning to Refine Dialogue History for Personalized Generation
keyInsights: Sparse extraction of high-value tokens beats indiscriminate truncation
Three personal information refiners + personalized response generator
Similar users' dialogue patterns strengthen individual personalization

sources.title: User-Adaptive Meta-Learning for Cold-Start Medication Recommendation
keyInsights: Dual-adaptation: self (own history) + peer (similar users)
Uncertainty quantification filters unrelated support visits
Pattern for rapid new-user adaptation with minimal data

sources.title: Combating Data Scarcity: VARK Cognitive Profiling + LLMs
keyInsights: VARK categories (Visual/Auditory/Reading/Kinesthetic) for cold-start profiling
LLMs enrich sparse item descriptions for knowledge graph construction
6-component system handles cold start from minimal initial data

sources.title: Temporal Matrix Factorization for Tracking Concept Drift in User Preferences
keyInsights: Linear transition model tracks preference vector changes over time
5-20% RMSE improvement for users with genuine preference drift
Automatically identifies users experiencing preference shifts

sources.title: The AI Hippocampus: How Far are We From Human Memory?
keyInsights: Three paradigms: implicit (in-weights), explicit (external store), agentic (persistent temporal)
Episodic-like memory in agents via temporal sequences
Semantic-like memory via distributed parameter knowledge

sources.title: Matrix Factorization for Recommendation (Google ML Crash Course)
keyInsights: Weighted Matrix Factorization handles unobserved interactions
Dot product similarity biases toward popular items (use cosine for pure relevance)
WALS converges faster than SGD for recommendation tasks

sources.title: Candidate Generation (Google ML Recommendation)
keyInsights: Two-stage: broad candidate generation → personalized ranking
Similarity metric choice embeds behavioral biases
Content-based vs collaborative filtering tradeoffs

sources.title: Patterns for LLM Applications (Eugene Yan)
keyInsights: Data flywheel: user feedback enables continuous improvement
Explicit (ratings) vs implicit (code acceptance) feedback patterns
Defensive UX: set realistic expectations to build trust despite LLM imperfection
88% of respondents consider retrieval a key stack component (Sequoia survey)

sources.title: Contextual Retrieval (Anthropic)
keyInsights: 49% fewer retrieval failures with contextual embeddings + BM25
67% fewer failures with reranking added
Prepend context to chunks before embedding to preserve meaning

sources.title: Mem0 Travel Assistant Example
keyInsights: memory.add(messages, user_id) + memory.search(query, user_id) pattern
text-embedding-3-large for semantic memory matching
Per-user isolation via user_id; preferences persist cross-session

sources.title: Conversational Memory for LLMs (Pinecone)
keyInsights: Buffer (full) vs Summary (compressed) vs Window (recent k) vs Hybrid memory types
SummaryBuffer hybrid: summarize old, keep recent raw — best of both
max_token_limit tuning key for hybrid approaches
